prompt: A devastating, hyper-realistic aerial photograph looking straight down at a vast field of hundreds of tiny worn children's shoes arranged in a dense grid pattern on dry, cracked red-brown earth reminiscent of sub-Saharan Africa. The shoes are faded, dusty, weathered — tiny sandals, small sneakers, baby booties — each one representing a child lost to preventable disease. They stretch endlessly toward the horizon in muted desaturated tones of grey-brown. In the lower-right quadrant, one small circular cluster of about 15-20 shoes are brand new, brightly colored (red, yellow, blue, green), pristine, arranged around a single small clear glass vaccine vial filled with bright golden liquid sitting upright in the center of the circle. This small bright cluster is lit by warm golden sunlight while the rest of the frame is in overcast shadow. The scale is overwhelming — hundreds of empty worn shoes versus the tiny circle of colorful new ones. The vaccine vial anchors the meaning: these deaths were preventable with medicine that exists. The children's shoes make it unmistakably about children. The red earth grounds it in the regions most affected. No text. No labels. No charts. No UI elements. Pure photographic editorial style. Shot from directly above like a drone photograph. Emotionally devastating. Magazine cover quality. 4K resolution. Single frame.
dataset_name: Child Mortality Estimates - UN Inter-agency Group for Child Mortality Estimation (UNICEF, WHO, World Bank, UN DESA)
insight: Nearly 5 million children under five still die each year from largely preventable causes, despite a 60% decline in global child mortality since 1990.
